title: Homer The Father

jokes.text: Tonight on 'Kent's Korrections,' it has been brought to our attention that the word 'correction' is actually spelled with a 'C.' We regret any confusion this may have caused.
jokes.punchline: Newscaster having to correct his own show's misspelling of 'correction'
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about the absurdity of misspelling a word about corrections on a corrections show. Well-structured irony with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Welcome back to Tube Town, the home of Sheriff Wholesome; Black President... Failed Geena Davis Sitcom 1986... The Voiceover Years... Hannigan, M.F.A.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd show titles including 'Failed Geena Davis Sitcom 1986'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and specificity in the absurd TV channel lineup, particularly the meta reference to failed sitcoms.

jokes.text: Filmed before a live studio audience. Everyone in that studio audience is dead now.
jokes.setup: Standard sitcom disclaimer about live audience
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact statement about audience mortality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy with perfect deadpan delivery. Takes a mundane disclaimer and adds existential dread with surgical precision.

jokes.text: Oh, life was so much easier when a machine told you when to laugh.
jokes.setup: Homer watching old sitcom with laugh track
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic comment about laugh tracks as emotional guidance
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on media consumption and emotional dependency. Very Homer-esque perspective.

jokes.text: I liked the producing, the executive producing and especially the co-producing. But the supervising producing was the best I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on TV show credits
jokes.punchline: Treating producer titles as entertainment content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer absurdity - missing the point entirely and focusing on meaningless details with sincere enthusiasm.

jokes.text: I think I just met the thing I'm gonna die on.
jokes.punchline: Expressing love through death imagery
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy mixing childhood desire with morbid humor. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I've found my new look. Those are girl overalls. I could pull it off.
jokes.punchline: Confident he can wear girl's clothing
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's confidence and lack of gender hang-ups in a believable character moment.

jokes.text: Because when you get anything you want, you don't want anything you get.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to sound wise about parenting
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that sounds profound but is meaningless
jokes.explanation: Perfect pseudo-wisdom from Homer - sounds like it means something but completely circular. Great character integration.

jokes.text: What is this crap? Are you wearing a wire?
jokes.punchline: Bart's paranoid conspiracy theory explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Bart's suspicious nature and how out-of-character good parenting sounds from Homer.

jokes.text: I'd rather take my chances in a hot car than go in a store with you.
jokes.punchline: Preferring deadly danger to shopping with dad
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good escalation - comparing parental embarrassment to literal death threats.

jokes.text: What Bart needs is for you to strangle him with your love.
jokes.punchline: Using 'strangle' metaphorically after literal strangling context
jokes.explanation: Clever callback to Homer's signature move, recontextualized as loving advice. Good wordplay.

jokes.text: I never thought of fatherhood as something that could affect a kid.
jokes.setup: Homer having a realization about parenting
jokes.punchline: Completely missing the fundamental point of parenting
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer logic - profound ignorance delivered with sincere revelation. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: How about this? I let you punch me in the face and you get me a snowmobile.
jokes.punchline: Offering violence in exchange for getting Homer a gift
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and misdirection - starts with self-sacrifice but ends with getting Homer something better.

jokes.text: Marge, not in front of the refrigerator.
jokes.punchline: Homer's food priorities interrupting intimate moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - food obsession trumping romance. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Lisa was using her solar calculator yesterday, so I opened the drapes to let in more sun.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to impress Marge with good parenting
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding how solar calculators work
jokes.explanation: Good Homer absurdity - trying to help but fundamentally misunderstanding technology in a sweet way.

jokes.text: Well, maybe your kids didn't.
jokes.setup: Homer hoping kids didn't hear them having sex
jokes.punchline: Implying Marge's kids did hear
jokes.explanation: Dark implication delivered with perfect economy. Suggests family dysfunction with minimal words.

jokes.text: Don't you minimize me!
jokes.setup: Lisa presumably minimizing a computer window
jokes.punchline: Bart taking computer action personally
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mixing computer terminology with personal feelings. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: Kids don't deliver newspapers anymore. It's just creeps in trucks.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about newspaper delivery jobs
jokes.punchline: Modern reality of adult newspaper delivery being ominous
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about changing childhood jobs with dark implications about strangers.

jokes.text: I've been doing the family's books for years. I take what I need.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining how she gets money for grades
jokes.punchline: Casual admission to embezzlement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - using intelligence for mild corruption while maintaining moral superiority.

jokes.text: You just said, 'My car is full of ice cream.'
jokes.setup: Homer trying to sound sophisticated with French
jokes.punchline: Lisa correcting his nonsensical translation
jokes.explanation: Classic dynamic - Homer's pretentious incompetence met with Lisa's precise correction.

jokes.text: There's no way that means anything other than I get a minibike.
jokes.setup: Homer's vague promise about TV show rewards
jokes.punchline: Bart's confident misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Sets up dramatic irony - audience knows Bart is wrong but he's completely confident.

jokes.text: 'Tis the playground ghost! I swear I didn't mean to kill ya!
jokes.setup: Willie startled by Bart in playground
jokes.punchline: Immediate confession to accidental murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie moment - immediately jumping to supernatural guilt and casually admitting to killing someone.

jokes.text: How could you mistake him for me? It was that sassy mouth that got ye killed in the first place.
jokes.setup: Willie realizing Bart isn't a ghost
jokes.punchline: Explaining the murder was due to Bart's attitude
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - not only did Willie kill someone, but it was specifically because of their personality.

jokes.text: the feeling of accomplishment you can only get from hard work.
jokes.punchline: Homer giving hollow motivational speech instead
jokes.explanation: Standard disappointed expectation joke. Homer's attempt at wisdom falls flat appropriately.

jokes.text: Well, if they care that much about whether you have a minibike, then they weren't really your friends.
jokes.punchline: Homer's tone-deaf advice about friendship
jokes.explanation: Homer's misguided attempt at wisdom, missing the point about childhood social dynamics.

jokes.text: Hmm, if this were a TV show, that would be a hell of an act break. But it could use a button.
jokes.punchline: Homer analyzing it as television structure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about TV writing structure, breaking fourth wall while showing Homer's TV obsession.

jokes.text: Yah still see that crow on shows sometimez.
jokes.punchline: Willie's observation about aging actors
jokes.explanation: Willie's unexpected pop culture commentary with his characteristic accent and perspective.

jokes.text: Can't lose that. It's the best picture of me I have.
jokes.setup: Homer concerned about losing security badge
jokes.punchline: Caring about it for vanity rather than security
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - audience expects security concern but gets Homer's typical narcissism.

jokes.text: America's enemies would give anything for your nuclear knowledge. Yeah, I do know a lot about nuclears.
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident mispronunciation of 'nuclear'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - confident ignorance demonstrated through mispronunciation while claiming expertise.

jokes.text: And America has so many Iran, Iraq, China, Mordor, the hoochies that laid low Tiger Woods...
jokes.punchline: Including fictional and ridiculous 'enemies'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation mixing real countries with Lord of the Rings and Tiger Woods scandal. Classic Homer confusion.

jokes.text: Homer, you can't order anything from that magazine. It's 25 years old.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to order from old TV Guide
jokes.punchline: Marge pointing out the obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for Homer's obliviousness. Marge as voice of reason.

jokes.text: A gutter cleaning coupon? Hey, they're your gutters, too.
jokes.punchline: Marge's defensive response about shared property
jokes.explanation: Mild family humor about shared responsibilities. Marge trying to make junk mail seem relevant.

jokes.text: Another disgrace for the French Bureau of Investigation.
jokes.punchline: French agent claiming it as their failure
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on FBI acronym with French twist. Mild international humor.

jokes.text: Don't forget us, the A-Team of Finland. I pity the fool who messes with B.A. Backstrom.
jokes.punchline: Finnish A-Team with culturally appropriate name
jokes.explanation: Creative cultural adaptation of Mr. T's catchphrase with Scandinavian name. Good international humor.

jokes.text: You pledge allegiance to the flag. And the flag is made in China.
jokes.punchline: Agent pointing out manufacturing irony
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about globalization irony. Economical and pointed political commentary.

jokes.text: Crap, total crap. You, writer, what's your name? Um, David Mamet. Well, Mr. Mamet, why don't you learn how to write a script!
jokes.punchline: Insulting prestigious playwright David Mamet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about Hollywood ignorance. Insulting one of America's greatest playwrights shows perfect TV actor pretension.

jokes.text: He's buying it. I'm buying it!
jokes.setup: Bart manipulating Homer with fake affection
jokes.punchline: Both characters simultaneously acknowledging the deception
jokes.explanation: Good moment of mutual awareness in their manipulation game

jokes.text: 'Cause you can wake me up if somebody comes.
jokes.setup: Homer happy to have Bart at work
jokes.punchline: Admitting he plans to sleep on the job
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer work ethic. Treating son as lookout for napping shows his priorities and parenting style.

jokes.text: Check this out, Bart. One of those retinal scan dealies. It only opens to my eye or an exact copy.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning the security flaw
jokes.explanation: Good setup for later payoff. Homer's casual mention of security vulnerability shows typical lack of awareness.

jokes.text: Ooh! Damn, that hurt! I'll just use my eye.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to copy Homer's eye scan
jokes.punchline: Pain from pressing eye against scanner
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy. Predictable outcome but sets up espionage plot device.

jokes.text: Sweet dreams, boy. They will be if you're in 'em, Pops.
jokes.punchline: Bart's overly sweet response as part of his manipulation
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable sweetness that shows Bart's manipulation getting creepy

jokes.text: Aw, they gave me my own vanity plate.
jokes.punchline: License plate presumably with his name or something childish
jokes.explanation: Mild visual humor about personalization. Shows foreign agents' attention to detail or Bart's excitement.

jokes.text: I wouldn't trade it for all the tea in... China...
jokes.punchline: Realizing the irony of mentioning China given his spy situation
jokes.explanation: Good moment of realization. Bart's guilt manifests through accidentally relevant idiom.

jokes.text: You betrayed your country for no reason. Now you're gonna see stars and stripes forever!
jokes.punchline: Threatening him with patriotic imagery/violence
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay mixing patriotic imagery with threat of violence. Standard spy movie escalation.

jokes.text: I've got summer squash!
jokes.punchline: Marge's completely unrelated domestic announcement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comic relief cutting through tension with pure domestic normalcy. Perfect Marge obliviousness.

jokes.text: Well, maybe you shouldn't listen to a 30-year-old TV show that only got on the air 'cause the creator had evidence the network president ran over a guy.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about TV parenting advice not working
jokes.punchline: Marge revealing dark truth about show's production
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy about Hollywood corruption. Subverts expectation of wholesome family TV with criminal blackmail.

jokes.text: I can't believe a boy who would betray his country would betray a different country. Unbelievable! Terrible!
jokes.punchline: Agent's moral outrage at betraying betrayers
jokes.explanation: Good irony about criminal ethics. Spy's indignation at betrayal while being betrayers themselves.

jokes.text: A teddy bear?! A teddy bear made in China.
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's a children's toy from China
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation and callback to manufacturing theme. Subverts weapon expectation with innocent toy.

jokes.text: The boy's flash drive may be in there, but I've got a lifetime of nuclear secrets up here.
jokes.punchline: Claiming vast nuclear knowledge despite proven incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's delusional confidence about his expertise despite constant evidence of ignorance.

jokes.text: Okay, but don't read our subtitles.
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about subtitle reading
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke breaking fourth wall about subtitle conventions. Acknowledges medium while maintaining story.

jokes.text: I know what you're saying. I understand food talk in every language!
jokes.setup: Agents speaking foreign language about food
jokes.punchline: Homer's specialized linguistic ability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his food obsession creates unexpected expertise. Brilliant character integration.

jokes.text: Okay, who can take me to the airport for less than $20?
jokes.setup: Homer in foreign country after nuclear plant situation
jokes.punchline: Casual request for cheap transportation
jokes.explanation: Good understatement - treating international incident aftermath as minor inconvenience with budget concerns.

jokes.text: Well, now it's time for the best kind of bonding-- sitting next to each other in silence, staring blankly at the TV.
jokes.punchline: Homer's definition of quality time
jokes.explanation: Perfect observational humor about modern family dynamics. Homer's honest assessment of quality time.

jokes.text: I slept with everyone in the cast, including the dog.
jokes.punchline: Extreme oversharing including bestiality
jokes.explanation: Standard shock humor escalation. Goes for extreme rather than clever, but fits the behind-the-scenes expose format.

jokes.text: To quote Matthew Perry from Friends, 'too much information.' And to quote Dwayne from What's Happening, 'Hey... Hey... Hey.'
jokes.punchline: Mixing wrong actor with wrong show then irrelevant catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good confusion of pop culture references showing host's incompetence and nervous deflection.

jokes.text: My favorite song now. Sorry, 'Don't Fear The Reaper.'
jokes.punchline: Casual apology to previous favorite song
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - treating song preference change like personal betrayal. Shows his simple emotional attachments.

jokes.text: Everybody! Air guitar! Come on, do it with me. Use your whammy bar! Whoo! Now fuzz it up!
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic air guitar performance with technical instructions
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer enthusiasm taking things too far. His detailed air guitar instructions show characteristic over-investment.

tmdb_overview: When Homer insists Bart must earn the new dirt bike he wants, Bart comes up with a scheme; James Lipton interviews a TV cast.
